description_long: This object is a small, ornate pagoda structure with a prominent, dark-tiled roof featuring pronounced, upward-curving eaves at each corner. The roof is supported by a square stone base, and the central section displays a decorative medallion or crest on each gable. The roof tiles are arranged in neat, overlapping rows, and the eaves extend outward, giving the structure a sense of elegance and lightness. The base appears to be made of stone or concrete, with a slightly raised platform and a circular pedestal at the center. The overall design is reminiscent of traditional East Asian architecture, with careful attention to symmetry and ornamental detail.
description: A small, ornate East Asian pagoda with a dark, tiled roof and upward-curving eaves, set on a square stone base with decorative medallions.
description_view_0: Front view showing the decorative medallion on the gable and the symmetrical, upward-curving eaves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered roof tiles and the pronounced outward curve of the eaves.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with another decorative medallion and the same roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the roof's curvature and the structure's symmetry.
